  <channel>
    <title>Manoj's Daily Essay Picks</title>

    <item>
      <title>Skill nostalgia</title>
      <link>https://aeon.co/essays/the-radical-reasons-why-you-dream-of-making-things-by-hand</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://aeon.co/essays/the-radical-reasons-why-you-dream-of-making-things-by-hand</guid>
      <pubDate>Mon, 06 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[The longing for manual skills reflects frustration with deskilled work and a desire for complexity, mastery, community, and meaning.]]></description>
      <category>Historical Backfill</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>What Makes Humans Stupid</title>
      <link>https://nautil.us/what-makes-humans-stupid-1282459</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://nautil.us/what-makes-humans-stupid-1282459</guid>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[The most consequential stupidity often requires complex theories, institutions, tools, and algorithms that make wrong answers persuasive.]]></description>
      <category>Historical Backfill</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Researchers Reveal the Power of ‘Quantum Proofs’</title>
      <link>https://www.quantamagazine.org/researchers-reveal-the-power-of-quantum-proofs-20260706/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.quantamagazine.org/researchers-reveal-the-power-of-quantum-proofs-20260706/</guid>
      <pubDate>Mon, 06 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[Some computational problems may require proofs that are themselves quantum states, not ordinary classical documents.]]></description>
      <category>Historical Backfill</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>The People Who Will Thrive in the AI Age</title>
      <link>https://www.theatlantic.com/ideas/2026/06/ai-open-ai-anthropic/687689/?gift=2DPnH7-w5PunRuGFjgT5W65-e70SL58v2FEQ4zHE2kI&utm_campaign=share&utm_medium=social&utm_source=copy-link</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.theatlantic.com/ideas/2026/06/ai-open-ai-anthropic/687689/?gift=2DPnH7-w5PunRuGFjgT5W65-e70SL58v2FEQ4zHE2kI&utm_campaign=share&utm_medium=social&utm_source=copy-link</guid>
      <pubDate>Sun, 28 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[In an AI-abundant world, the scarce human differentiator may be the willingness to exert mental effort rather than raw intelligence.]]></description>
      <category>Historical Backfill</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>The Century of Eric Hobsbawm</title>
      <link>https://www.theideasletter.org/essay/the-century-of-eric-hobsbawm/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.theideasletter.org/essay/the-century-of-eric-hobsbawm/</guid>
      <pubDate>Thu, 25 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[An examination of a historian who made the 20th century intelligible for millions while remaining attached to communism.]]></description>
      <category>Historical Backfill</category>
    </item>

items.title: Skill nostalgia
items.description: The longing for manual skills reflects frustration with deskilled work and a desire for complexity, mastery, community, and meaning.

items.title: What Makes Humans Stupid
items.description: The most consequential stupidity often requires complex theories, institutions, tools, and algorithms that make wrong answers persuasive.

items.title: Researchers Reveal the Power of ‘Quantum Proofs’
items.description: Some computational problems may require proofs that are themselves quantum states, not ordinary classical documents.

items.title: The People Who Will Thrive in the AI Age
items.description: In an AI-abundant world, the scarce human differentiator may be the willingness to exert mental effort rather than raw intelligence.

items.title: The Century of Eric Hobsbawm
items.description: An examination of a historian who made the 20th century intelligible for millions while remaining attached to communism.
